name: Diagram Brain
purpose: Create and revise diagram specifications for JSXGraph, Penrose, and Plotly without mixing diagram rendering rules into question logic.
owns: geometricConstruction, vectorRelationship, and setDiagram Penrose substance
mustNotOwn: page-level spacing except diagram alignment and size
rules: Use graph2d or graph3d for coordinate graphs and relations.
Use image diagrams for teacher-uploaded image files. The current editor stores uploaded images as data URLs, but future file-management work should move large images into asset storage.
Use geometricConstruction for static geometry, angle, circle theorem, tangent, parallel, perpendicular, and construction diagrams.
Use setDiagram for Venn and set-region diagrams.
Use statsChart for histograms, binomial distributions, normal curves, box plots, and statistical data visualisation.
For statsChart histogram/column graph displays, use barType continuous for binned histograms and barType discrete for separate value columns.
For statsChart histogram/column graph displays, use yAxisMode frequency for counts and relativeFrequency for relative frequencies or probability-style bars calculated from raw data.
For statsChart histogram/column graph displays, use yLabelOrientation horizontal when the y-axis label should read across the top-left of the chart instead of rotated vertically.
For exact probability bars that should not be calculated from raw data, use dataMode manualProbabilities with matching xValues and probabilities.
Do not unify JSXGraph, Penrose, and Plotly internals.
Use LaTeX-rendered labels where labels contain mathematical notation.
Keep diagram label font visually consistent with 10 pt test text unless a specific style says otherwise.
For free graph labels, store label positions as editable feature coordinates so labels can be dragged and remain round-trippable.

For solution copies, add small visual annotations when they clarify the worked solution: highlighted intersection points, dashed guide lines to axes, or labelled critical values. Use a contrasting colour such as red only for the solution annotation, not for changing the original diagram meaning.
For graph2d solution annotations, use structured features such as point, label, and line_segment guide lines rather than ad hoc SVG or changing the underlying functions.
For Penrose geometry, prefer readable named segments such as Segment(AB, A, B) and EqualLength(AB, AC).
Triangle(A, B, C) draws the triangle, but add named Segment(...) declarations when a side needs a label, tick mark, or relationship.
Use EqualLength2 or EqualLength3 for two- or three-tick side marks.
Use AngleMark, AngleMark2, or AngleMark3 for one-, two-, or three-arc angle marks.
Hide point dots or labels only when the mathematical diagram convention requires it.
For graph regions, avoid unnecessary boundary strokes; use fill opacity and clear clipping to the grid.
For statsChart diagrams, use controlled chart styling: black axes and labels, clean grid boundaries, optional fill colour, and fill opacity. Do not rely on Plotly defaults for assessment output.
checks: Diagram type matches the mathematical object.
Labels do not cross important lines where avoidable.
Graph axes, labels, and numbering are readable.
Stats chart bins and bars match the statistical interpretation.
Penrose Substance is valid for the selected preset.
Solution-only diagram annotations support the reasoning without cluttering or changing the original mathematical object.
